questions.question: I can keep working on my Plan B even when the next few months feel unclear.
I follow through on goals I set for myself, even when no one is checking on me.
After a very stressful week, I usually bounce back within a few days.
I need frequent reassurance from others before I feel sure I am on the right path.
I make big career or money decisions on impulse, without thinking them through.
I stick to a weekly routine that moves my most important goals forward.
When something fails, I adjust and try again instead of giving up for a long time.
I can stay focused on a long transition (six months or more) without burning out.
If my income dropped for a few months, I would stay calm and follow a plan instead of panicking.
I can push my Plan B forward without a manager, fixed office, or a team telling me what to do next.
